summary: Asset ledger now covers 5 sources and 18 selected assets, including deterministic modified Kenney-derived metal textures generated for the foundation floor, walls, and roof.
observations: docs/assets/source-ledger.json records source, license, hashes, byte counts, and load group.
src/game/weapons/weaponManifest.ts exposes the runtime weapon definitions for the selector and loader.
The first weapon payload is 164055B for GLB model data plus the shared color texture, before previews and metadata.
publicAssetUrl() appends assetBuild to preview and model paths.
GLTFLoader uses LoadingManager.setURLModifier(withAssetVersion) so GLB-referenced textures also receive assetBuild.
Kenney Prototype Textures are registered as the first foundation environment texture source.
The active foundation environment texture payload is 764229B across derivative metal floor, wall, and roof PNGs.
publicAssetUrl and withAssetVersion now live in src/game/assetUrls.ts and are re-exported by the weapon manifest for compatibility.
validate-assets checks git tracking with git ls-files --error-unmatch so ledger files cannot pass by existing only on local disk.
Foundation texture records marked modified true preserve the original Kenney sourceSha256 and track the committed derivative under optimizedSha256 and sha256.
Added weapon.blaster.rift and weapon.blaster.torch to level-01-weapons.
Added Quaternius monster GLBs to foundation-enemies.
Added RIFT, TORCH, and 48 second music records to generated audio metadata.
Added OpenAI-generated title image provenance to the ledger.
Derivative foundation metal textures are generated by scripts/generate-foundation-metal-textures.mjs and stay in the foundation-environment load group.
Generated metal foundation textures are reproducible through scripts/generate-foundation-metal-textures.mjs.
Latest npm run validate:assets passed after recording the derivative texture bytes and hashes.
decisions: Use level-01-weapons as the first load group.
Keep source/provenance metadata outside runtime code.
Add npm run validate:assets as the first file/hash registry gate.
Use the build id query parameter for public assets that are not processed by Vite filename hashing.
Use foundation-environment as the first environment texture load group.
Allow modified CC0 texture derivatives only when source and committed hashes are both recorded.
caughtIssues: A generalized registry/playground UI still does not exist.
Cache invalidation depends on public hosts preserving query strings.
risks: Runtime and ledger metadata can drift if new imports bypass validate-assets.
Texture assets bypass preview validation, so screenshot QA must confirm readability.
New asset files must be staged or committed before validate-assets can pass locally.
